content: 2024-2025 state-of-the-art active learning agent memory systems can be optimized by tiering curiosity-driven exploration signals from intrinsic curiosity modules (ICM) and random network distillation (RND) directly into episodic memory storage priority, where only high-curiosity state transitions get retained for long-term planning rather than uniformly storing all encountered experiences. This creates a self-pruning memory architecture where curiosity acts as a built-in active selection signal that aligns with the core active learning goal of prioritizing the most informative data.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
